title: sorbus intermedia
intro: Sorbus intermedia, also known as the Larch or Middle European Whitebeam, is a deciduous tree or shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to help with wound healing, fever reduction, and pain relief. The health benefits of Sorbus intermedia include its potential to act as an antiviral agent, helping to combat infections and alleviate symptoms associated with the common cold and flu. Sorbus intermedia can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in the production of traditional folk medicines in various parts of the world.
